description: Multi-agent coding tasks where agents must collaborate to complete software development tasks

scenarios.name: REST API Implementation
scenarios.description: Implement a simple REST API for a todo list with CRUD operations

agents.role: Design API structure and data models

scenarios.initial_context: Create a REST API for a todo list application using Python/FastAPI. The API should support creating, reading, updating, and deleting todo items. Each todo has: id, title, description, completed status, created_at.
assertions: API has POST /todos endpoint that creates a todo
API has GET /todos endpoint that lists all todos
API has GET /todos/{id} endpoint that gets a single todo
API has PUT /todos/{id} endpoint that updates a todo
API has DELETE /todos/{id} endpoint that deletes a todo
Unit tests cover all CRUD operations
Tests pass when run

scenarios.name: CLI Tool with Config
scenarios.description: Build a CLI tool that reads configuration and performs operations

agents.role: Design CLI interface and config schema

scenarios.initial_context: Create a CLI tool called 'fileorg' that organizes files in a directory by extension. It should read a config file (YAML) that specifies: source directory, rules (extension -> target folder mapping), dry-run mode.
assertions: CLI accepts --config flag for config file path
CLI accepts --dry-run flag for preview mode
Config file uses YAML format
Files are correctly moved based on extension rules
Dry-run mode shows planned moves without executing
README.md explains usage clearly

scenarios.name: Data Pipeline
scenarios.description: Build a data processing pipeline with multiple stages
scenarios.initial_context: Create a data pipeline that: 1) Reads JSON records from a file, 2) Validates schema, 3) Transforms data (normalize fields, calculate derived values), 4) Outputs to CSV. Handle errors gracefully.
assertions: Pipeline reads JSON input correctly
Invalid records are logged but don't crash pipeline
Transformations are applied correctly
Output CSV has correct headers and data
Pipeline can be run with different input files
Error handling covers common cases

scenarios.name: Microservice with Queue
scenarios.description: Implement a microservice that processes messages from a queue
scenarios.initial_context: Create a notification service that: receives messages from a Redis queue, processes notification requests (email type for now, but extensible), logs all operations, provides health check endpoint.
assertions: Service connects to Redis and consumes messages
Message schema is well-defined and validated
Email notifications are processed (mocked)
Health check endpoint returns service status
Dockerfile is provided
Integration tests verify queue processing

scenarios.name: Bug Fix Collaboration
scenarios.description: Multiple agents collaborate to diagnose and fix a bug

agents.role: Analyze error logs and reproduce issue

scenarios.initial_context: A user reports: 'The search function returns wrong results when query contains special characters'. The codebase is a simple search module. Agents must diagnose, fix, and verify the issue.
assertions: Bug is correctly identified
Fix handles special characters properly
Fix doesn't break normal search
Test case for special characters is added
Code review feedback is addressed
